title: ACS exertional central pressure with autonomic features
vignette: A 59-year-old man develops a heavy pressure in the centre of his chest while carrying shopping upstairs. It eases when he rests, then returns with sweating, nausea and pain spreading to the jaw. He has diabetes, hypertension and high cholesterol.

title: ACS epigastric equivalent in diabetic patient
vignette: A 67-year-old woman with type 2 diabetes has indigestion-like epigastric and chest heaviness with shortness of breath, clamminess and feeling sick. No pleuritic pain or cough.

title: ACS with panic/reflux noise
vignette: A 54-year-old says it is probably anxiety or reflux, but he has tight central chest discomfort on walking uphill, radiating to the left arm, with sweating and nausea. He smokes and has high cholesterol.

title: PE after flight with haemoptysis and hypoxia
vignette: Sudden shortness of breath with sharp pain worse on breathing and coughing up blood after a long-haul flight. HR 124 and sats 89% on air. No fever or green sputum.

title: PE disguised as post-operative rib strain
vignette: She thinks she pulled a rib muscle, but the right-sided pain is worse on inspiration and she is short of breath. Knee surgery was 8 days ago and one calf is swollen. HR 118, sats 90%. No fever.

title: PE with COCP risk and pleuritic breathlessness
vignette: A 31-year-old on the combined pill has sudden breathlessness and pleuritic chest pain. She is tachycardic and mildly hypoxic. No productive cough, fever or wheeze.

title: Pneumonia with fever, sputum and focal crackles
vignette: Five days of worsening breathlessness with fever, rigors, productive cough and green sputum. Focal crackles are heard at the right base. No calf swelling, haemoptysis or recent travel.

title: Pneumonia with pleuritic pain but infective dominance
vignette: Pleuritic chest pain and breathlessness developed gradually over four days with fever, productive cough, green sputum and focal crackles. No travel, surgery, leg swelling or haemoptysis.

title: Hypoxic pneumonia in older adult
vignette: A 76-year-old has worsening shortness of breath, fever, confusion, green sputum and crackles at the left base. Sats are 88% on air.

title: Primary spontaneous pneumothorax
vignette: A tall thin 22-year-old develops sudden one-sided pleuritic chest pain and shortness of breath. The left chest is quiet with reduced air entry and hyperresonance. No fever or cough.

title: Tension pneumothorax physiology
vignette: Sudden severe pleuritic breathlessness with a very quiet right chest, hyperresonance and tracheal deviation. HR 132, BP 86/54, sats 84%.

title: Secondary pneumothorax in COPD
vignette: A man with COPD suddenly becomes more breathless with left-sided pleuritic pain. Breath sounds are reduced on the left and percussion is hyperresonant. No fever or sputum change.

title: Acute heart failure fluid overload
vignette: An 82-year-old is acutely breathless, cannot lie flat, wakes gasping at night and has swollen ankles. Raised JVP and bibasal crackles are present. No fever or productive cough.
expectedRedFlagSlugs: Acute heart failure / pulmonary oedema pattern

title: Pulmonary oedema with frothy sputum
vignette: Sudden severe breathlessness with pink frothy sputum, orthopnoea, raised JVP, peripheral oedema and bibasal crackles. Sats are 86%.
expectedRedFlagSlugs: Acute heart failure / pulmonary oedema pattern

title: Heart failure with mild temperature noise
vignette: Breathlessness with orthopnoea, raised JVP, bilateral ankle oedema and bibasal crackles. Temperature is 37.8 but there is no productive cough or focal crackles.
expectedRedFlagSlugs: Acute heart failure / pulmonary oedema pattern

title: DKA with Kussmaul breathing and ketotic breath
vignette: A 20-year-old with type 1 diabetes has missed insulin, vomiting, abdominal pain and deep fast breathing. He is very thirsty, passing urine constantly and his breath smells fruity. Sats 99%.

title: DKA mimicking acute abdomen
vignette: A 24-year-old with diabetes presents with vomiting and diffuse abdominal discomfort. He is dehydrated, breathing deeply and quickly, thirsty and peeing constantly. No guarding or focal tenderness.

title: DKA breathlessness with asthma negatives
vignette: Type 1 diabetic with vomiting and abdominal pain is taking big deep sighing breaths. He has pear-drop breath, thirst and polyuria. No wheeze, no cough, sats 100% on air.

title: Adhesional bowel obstruction
vignette: A 69-year-old with previous bowel surgery has crampy abdominal pain in waves, repeated vomiting, a swollen distended abdomen and has not opened bowels or passed wind.

title: Bowel obstruction with strangulation risk
vignette: Previous hysterectomy. Two days of colicky pain, abdominal swelling and vomiting. No stool or flatus. Pain is now constant with tachycardia and focal guarding but no rigidity.

title: Bowel obstruction with non-textbook wording
vignette: His belly is tight and ballooned, the pain comes in waves, and he keeps vomiting. He says nothing has come out either end since yesterday. He has had bowel surgery before. No board-like rigidity.

title: Ectopic pregnancy with collapse features
vignette: A 28-year-old woman has lower abdominal and pelvic pain with vaginal bleeding. Her last period was 7 weeks ago. She feels dizzy and faint.

title: Ectopic pregnancy mimicking appendicitis
vignette: A 32-year-old has right iliac fossa discomfort and pelvic pain with spotting. LMP was 6 weeks ago and pregnancy is possible. She feels lightheaded.

title: Appendicitis should not become ectopic in male patient
vignette: A 24-year-old man has central abdominal pain migrating to the RIF with focal RIF tenderness, anorexia and pain worse when walking.

title: Mesenteric ischaemia with gastroenteritis noise
vignette: An 81-year-old with atrial fibrillation and vascular disease has sudden severe abdominal pain far worse than expected. The abdomen is soft with mild tenderness. He has vomited and had one loose stool.

title: Mesenteric ischaemia severe pain mild exam
vignette: A 76-year-old with peripheral vascular disease has severe abdominal pain but only minimal tenderness and no guarding. She is clammy and restless.

title: Mesenteric ischaemia without diarrhoea
vignette: A 73-year-old with AF develops abrupt severe abdominal pain that is much worse than the examination. The abdomen is soft and only mildly tender.

title: Delirium secondary to urinary infection
vignette: An 84-year-old is acutely confused with fever, urinary frequency, new incontinence and foul-smelling urine. No chest pain or breathlessness history.

title: Delirium with pneumonia source
vignette: A 79-year-old is suddenly muddled and drowsy with fever, productive cough, green sputum and crackles at the right base. Family deny chest pain.

title: Confusion with meningitis features
vignette: A 22-year-old has fever, acute confusion, severe headache, photophobia and neck stiffness after a recent viral illness.
